title: 10 BPRP Reports
body_text:
10	BPRP Reports
This Chapter applies to BC and Ontario credit unions and other Central 1 clients. Financial institutions in other provinces, contact your provincial central for information on BPRP reports.
10.1	Overview of BPRP Reports
Two BPRP reports are available as follows:
The Bill Payment Remittance Processing Monthly Charge Report (ERMC) is provided to your financial institution. This report lists charges posted to your financial institution.
The Tracing Vendor Report (ERTV) is provided to the Corporate Creditor. This report lists remittances received from all payment channels and includes summary totals and individual payment details.
10.2	Accessing BPRP Reports
BPRP reports are available to Corporate Creditors and financial institutions on Central 1âs File and Report Exchange.
Depending on your province, you can locate the ERMC report in the BILL or ONBL file from the root directory. The file contains your financial institutionâs ERMC reports, which are generally available on the second business day of the month. If applicable, PDFs of the ERMC reports are available in the Bill_Pmt folder. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on accessing Central 1âs File and Report Exchange.
The ERTV reports for Corporate Creditors are available in the Billpay folder in Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. Your financial institution must provide Corporate Creditors with procedures for accessing the ERTV reports on the File and Report Exchange. See Section 6.8, Providing File and Report Exchange Procedures and File Formats to Corporate Creditors.
10.3	ERMC â Bill Payment Remittance Processing Monthly Charge Report
Description
The ERMC Report lists the charges posted to your financial institution for BPRP. Charges are posted on the first business day of the month, effective the last business day of the previous month.
Purpose
Financial institutions use this report to reconcile your current account with Central 1 and to charge Corporate Creditors for BPRP services.
Organization
The charges for each Corporate Creditor display on separate pages in the ERMC report. There is one report per Corporate Creditor. Item counts and dollar amounts are separated by payment type. Other BPRP fees are provided (e.g., subscription fees). See Figure 1. At the end of the ERMC report, a summary provides the vendor grand total charges and lists the electronic Lead Bank payment volumes and how they apply to the tiers. See Figure 2.
Distribution
The ERMC report is available on the second business day of the month. It is distributed to all participating financial institutions in text format. A report is produced for each transit number.
Figure 1: Sample ERMC Report
Figure 2: Summary Section on ERMC Report
10.4	ERTV â Tracing Vendor Report
Description
The ERTV Report provides summary totals and individual payment details.
Purpose
The Corporate Creditor uses the ERTV report to manually credit its customersâ accounts if it does not receive a data file from Central 1. The ERTV report provides a record of payments that can assist with trace requests.
Organization
The report is sorted by Corporate Creditor. One line per transaction is provided and is sorted by transaction date and source. Payment details also include the customerâs account number, amount of payment, and customer name. A summary of payment by type is provided. See Figure 3.
Distribution
The report is available to Corporate Creditors Monday to Friday.
Figure 3: Sample ERTV Tracing Vendor Report
END
